Grown Ups (2010)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $162,001,186Details
International Box Office $110,221,234
Worldwide Box Office $272,222,420
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $48,056,460
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $11,974,065
Total $60,030,525
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Five men who were best friends when they were young kids and now are getting together for the Fourth of July weekend to meet each others' families for the first time. Picking up where they left off, they discover why growing older doesn't mean growing up.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$40,506,564 (25.0% of total gross)
Legs:4.00 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:59.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$75,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,534 opening theaters/3,534 max. theaters, 6.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $245,568,341

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 25th, 2010 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: August 21st, 2020 (Wide), released as Grown Ups (2010) (re: 2020) (Australia)
December 8th, 2022 (Limited) (Australia)
Video Release: November 9th, 2010 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
January 17th, 2011 by Sony Pictures
MPA Rating: PG-13 for crude material including suggestive references, language and some male rear nudity
(Rating bulletin 2121, 5/19/2010)
Running Time: 102 minutes
Franchise: Grown Ups
Cast, crew, or production detail: Prologue
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Funeral Reunions, Mid-Life Crisis, Unemployed
Social setting: Fourth of July
Subgenre: Farcical / Slapstick Comedy
Time period setting: 1970s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Relativity Media, Happy Madison
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Five men who were best friends when they were young kids and now are getting together for the Fourth of July weekend to meet each others' families for the first time. Picking up where they left off, they discover why growing older doesn't mean growing up.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$40,506,564 (25.0% of total gross)
Legs:4.00 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:59.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$75,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,534 opening theaters/3,534 max. theaters, 6.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $245,568,341

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 25th, 2010 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: August 21st, 2020 (Wide), released as Grown Ups (2010) (re: 2020) (Australia)
December 8th, 2022 (Limited) (Australia)
Video Release: November 9th, 2010 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
January 17th, 2011 by Sony Pictures
MPA Rating: PG-13 for crude material including suggestive references, language and some male rear nudity
